text:
We are reviewing real client calls with our AI agent at Supersonik.

This is some of the highest leverage work we do.

Because the product is not just the technology behind it. The product is the experience the end user has during the call.

That is what they judge.
That is what they remember.
That is what they associate with Supersonik.

Users do not care how hard the system is to build.
They care whether the experience feels smooth, useful, clear, and trustworthy.

So when we sit together and review recordings, we are not doing it as a ritual.
We are doing it because this is where the truth is.

The end user’s experience is the product.
And their perception is the brand.

If we want Supersonik to be great, we need to keep watching, learning, and improving those moments again and again.

text:
Anthropic shipped an absurd amount in 52 days.

We’re seeing the same pattern internally. After adopting AI aggressively, our engineering team is shipping +3x faster.

But AI does not remove bottlenecks. It shifts them.
The bottleneck is no longer writing code. It is PR reviews, observability, test coverage, and safe rollout.

Once code becomes cheap to produce, quality can no longer depend on humans manually catching everything in review. That does not scale.

So the stack has to change:
- stronger testing pipelines
- better observability
- canary deployments
- tighter feedback loops after release

This is the only way to move fast without hurting reliability.

For enterprise products, that matters even more. Customers want speed, but they also expect strong SLAs and predictable behavior.
AI is not just making teams faster. It is forcing teams to become much better at the discipline of shipping.

That is the real shift.

text:
TurboQuant from Google Research is suddenly everywhere this week, but the paper actually dropped back in 2025.

Classic AI cycle: ignore → rediscover → “this changes everything.”

Still, the results are genuinely interesting.

The paper shows that for KV-cache quantization, TurboQuant achieves quality neutrality at 3.5 bits per channel, with only marginal degradation at 2.5 bits.

Why it matters: KV cache is one of the biggest bottlenecks in LLM inference. Compressing it efficiently can reduce memory pressure and improve throughput, especially for long-context workloads.

Will it magically make real-time AI feel instant? No.  
But it’s a meaningful step toward making LLM systems cheaper and more scalable.

Less hype, more signal. This one is worth paying attention to.

Achieves ~8x compression with near-lossless inner-product estimation, plus plugins for Ollama, LangChain & more. MIT licensed, with benchmarks: https://github.com/lakshmana64/turboquant-app | Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.19874

text:
About six months ago, Daniel Carmona Serrat, Joaquim Lecha, and I set out on a bold mission:
👉 Build an AI that can run a live product demo instantly, with no scheduling.

What sounded simple turned into one of the hardest technical challenges I've faced: making an AI that can control a browser interactively, orchestrate LLMs, speak and understand multiple languages, and still respond in under one second, all on a live video call.

Today, I'm proud to share that we've raised $5M, led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from incredible angels including Oriol Vinyals (DeepMind), Xavier (Xavi) Amatriain (Google), and others.
Built in Barcelona, running globally. 🚀

For me as an engineer, few things are more exciting than tackling these problems at the edge of what's possible. And we're just getting started.

If you are interested in solving the hardest problems on real-time AI, join us!

text:
Nestor (YC S22) is now Aldara!

We're thrilled to announce that our journey as Nestor has evolved into a fresh, new chapter - Aldara. This rebranding is more than just a name change; it's a reflection of our commitment to staying modern and professional, appealing to all generations.

Innovation drives us at Aldara. We cater to all our clients' needs - offering digital solutions for the tech-savvy, and simpler, approachable options for those less digitally inclined. Our new identity symbolizes this versatility: we're as digital as you want us to be.

We're excited about this new era, and we can't wait to share more. Stay tuned for what's coming next!
